app.title: OS Exam Trainer — HIT 61206
home.planHint: 7/10 questions are exact-term recall from a recurring bank; 3/10 are mechanical (fork / scheduling / disk) and worth guaranteed points. Drill the free points to reflex, memorize the bank, over-learn the traps.
home.howToTitle: ℹ️ How to use this app
home.howToBody:
• Sections (below): open one → read the short lesson, then flip flashcards. Tap ✓ Got it / ✗ Missed — missed cards come back automatically (spaced repetition). Press space to flip.
  • ⚡ Practice Generator (fork · scheduling · memory): infinite fresh problems with instantly-checked, verified answers.
  • 📝 Mock Exam: 10 random questions scored like the real exam · 🔁 Review Weak: only what you missed · 📅 By Year: every real past exam.
  • 🎯 Focus: shows only what is on your current-semester exam. Every card shows the Hebrew question + the code + an English hint.
  • Progress is saved on your device.
home.trapsTitle: ⚠️ The 3 traps that cost aces their last points
home.trapsBody:
• Who sets which bit: CPU/MMU sets reference & dirty bits; the OS clears the reference bit (Clock) & sets present/valid.
  • Critical-section conditions: Mutual Exclusion / Progress / Bounded Waiting. "Starvation" is a consequence, not a condition.
  • Scheduling identity: RR(quantum=∞)=FCFS; SJF=non-preemptive, SRTF=preemptive.
gen.disk.intro: A hard disk: capacity {cap}, {platters} platters ({surfaces} surfaces), sector size {sec}
gen.disk.explain: total sectors = capacity/sector = {total}; = cylinders×surfaces×sectors-per-track. Solve for the unknown.
gen.paging.levelsQ: Multilevel paging with {lv} levels. On a TLB miss, how many memory accesses?
gen.paging.levelsExplain: {lv} page-table levels + 1 access to the page = {ans}.
gen.paging.entriesQ: Virtual address {vb} bits, page size {pb}. Entries in a single-level page table?
gen.paging.framesQ: Physical address {vb} bits, page size {pb}. How many frames?
gen.fork.askPrints: How many lines does the program print?
gen.fork.askProcs: How many processes exist in total?
gen.fork.explainProcs: Total processes spawned: {procs}. Count = all processes.
gen.fork.explainPrints: Total processes spawned: {procs}. Of those, {prints} reach the printf().

years.sub: Pick an exam and take it question by question — reveal each answer, then mark Got it / Missed. Progress is saved.
